Private Credit Impulse Signal
← Back to dashboard

Private Credit Impulse Signal

Private credit impulse: momentum in bank and consumer credit growth.

As of 2036-10-01

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Private Credit Impulse score of 0.95 and a rising Bank Credit Z-score of 0.95, this setup aligns with Upward-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Trend continuation, not Mean reversion. (1)
  • The signal has entered a confirmed "Accelerating" regime, indicating a shift toward risk-on financing conditions. (1)
  • Conviction Band: High; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: Yes. Signal Stability Assessment: Improving; Threshold Proximity: Near; Revision Sensitivity: High. (1)

Methodology Applied:

  • A Composite value > 0.75 confirmed for two periods defines an "Accelerating" credit regime. (1)
  • Rising values imply a credit expansion and a risk-on bias in the underlying economy. (1)
  • Values within [0.60, 0.75] are treated as transition risks for imminent regime flips. (1)
  • Latest dataset observation: 2026-03-31. (1)

Key Dynamics:

  • Primary driver is the acceleration in commercial bank credit (Z-score 0.95) and business loans (Z-score 1.34 in Feb). (1)
  • The signal transitioned from "Stable" in January to "Accelerating" in February and March. (1)
  • Internal Conflict: March data relies heavily on bank credit as business/consumer loan sub-components are currently missing. (1)
  • Conditional Invalidation: A drop in the composite below 0.75 for two consecutive periods. (1)

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Sustained cyclical expansion supported by easing bank credit availability.
  • Upside risk: Sharp business loan demand spike triggering a mid-cycle credit boom.
  • Downside risk: Immediate reversal of bank credit growth due to rapid tightening or adverse revisions.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months) as credit impulses typically lead economic activity shifts. (1)
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: High, as confirmed regime shifts in credit velocity are historically strong macro leads. (1)

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs aggregate liquidity and demand impulses within the broader macro environment. (1)
  • Mechanism: Accelerating credit creation signals easing financing conditions and rising capital demand. (1)
  • Cycle position: Early/mid-cycle expansion (implied by accelerating credit growth). (1)

Regime Context:

  • Newly entered Accelerating regime as of February 2026, ending a 15-month "Stable" period. (1)
  • Direction of change: Strengthening momentum despite the slight dip in the March composite value. (1)

Model Limitations:

  • Frequent weekly revisions to bank data can cause late-stage regime shifts. (1)
  • Nominal growth figures may be distorted by high-inflation environments. (1)

Data & References:

  • Commercial Bank Credit (TOTBKCR), Business Loans (BUSLOANS), Consumer Credit (TOTALSL). (1)
  • Most influential: TOTBKCR annualised 6-month growth (0.067). (1)
  • Additional reliability: FRED H.8 release and NFIB Small Business Lending surveys.

Private Credit Impulse Chart

Momentum in U.S. bank credit, business lending, and consumer loans.
